name: Pic d'Escurets Loop
name_original: Col de Marie-Blanque et pic d'Escurets
description: Hike the moderate Pic d'Escurets Loop in Vallée d'Ossau for panoramic views from Escurets Peak and diverse Pyrenean scenery.
region_intro: Hiking around Bilhères offers diverse landscapes within the Pyrénées-Atlantiques department of southwestern France. The region features Pyrenean peaks, green mountains of the Ossau Valley, and the open pastures of the Plateau du Benou. Trails traverse varied terrain including woods, summer pasture areas, and near creeks, with some routes leading to ancient stone circles. Best hiking trails around Bilhères The most popular hiking route is Pic d'Escurets Loop, an 8.4 miles (13.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 30 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic views of the surrounding Pyrenean peaks. Another top favourite among local hikers is Plateau du Bénou – The Bénou Plateau loop from Bilhères, a moderate 4.8 miles (7.8 km) path. This trail explores the green Plateau du Benou, known for its pastures and scenic vistas. Local hikers also love the Small waterfall – Cromlechs of the Couraüs d'Accaüs loop from Bilhères, a 2.3 miles (3.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain to ancient stone circles, often completed in about 1 hour 10 minutes. Hiking around Bilhères is defined by Pyrenean peaks, green valleys, and ancient stone circles. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Bilhères are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 330 reviews. More than 2900 hikers have used komoot to explore Bilhères's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Bilhères?
A: There are over 30 hiking trails around Bilhères, offering a diverse range of experiences. This includes 11 easy routes, 16 moderate trails, and 5 more challenging options for experienced hikers.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Bilhères?
A: The hiking trails in Bilhères are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 330 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the magnificent 360-degree panoramic views of the Pyrenean peaks, the varied terrain including woods and pastures, and the unique historical sites like the ancient stone circles.

Q: Are there easy or family-friendly hiking options in Bilhères?
A: Yes, Bilhères offers several easy and family-friendly hikes. A popular choice is the walk to the ancient stone circles, which can be explored via the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6980164" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small waterfall – Cromlechs of the Couraüs d'Accaüs loop from Bilhères&lt;/a&gt;. This route is relatively short and leads through varied terrain to the enigmatic cromlechs, offering stunning valley views.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Bilhères?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more strenuous adventure, there are 5 difficult routes available. The region's Pyrenean peaks provide opportunities for challenging ascents with significant elevation gain, rewarding hikers with extensive panoramic views.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: Hiking around Bilhères offers a rich natural environment. Trails frequently traverse through varied terrain including dense woods, extensive summer pasture areas, and alongside creeks. You'll also encounter green mountains, the picturesque Ossau Valley, and the open landscapes of the Plateau du Benou, often with views of prominent peaks like Pic du Midi d'Ossau.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, a unique historical attraction in the area is the mysterious site of the cromlechs of Lou Couraus. These ancient stone circles bear witness to early inhabitants and can be explored on walks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6980164" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small waterfall – Cromlechs of the Couraüs d'Accaüs loop from Bilhères&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the region is dotted with traditional mountain huts and pastures.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Bilhères are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e871365357/pic-d-escurets-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic d'Escurets Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10526633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plateau du Bénou – The Bénou Plateau loop from Bilhères&lt;/a&gt;, which offer diverse scenery and return to their starting points.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or panoramic spots in the area?
A: Bilhères provides access to breathtaking views of the surrounding Pyrenean peaks. From summits like Pic d'Escurets, you can enjoy magnificent 360-degree panoramas of towns like Pau and Oloron-Sainte-Marie, as well as prominent mountains like Pic du Midi d'Ossau. The Montagne du Rey also offers superb panoramic views from the Pau plain to the Pic du Midi d'Ossau.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Bilhères?
A: The best time for hiking in Bilhères is generally from spring through autumn, when the weather is mild and the trails are clear. Summer offers lush green landscapes and warmer temperatures, while spring and autumn provide pleasant conditions with fewer crowds and beautiful seasonal colors. Some routes, like the Couscouilha Hut trail, are also suitable for snowshoeing in winter.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to or pass by water features like waterfalls?
A: Yes, some trails in the area feature water elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6980164" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small waterfall – Cromlechs of the Couraüs d'Accaüs loop from Bilhères&lt;/a&gt; specifically mentions a small waterfall as a point of interest. Additionally, many routes traverse near creeks and streams, especially in the wooded and pasture areas.

Q: Can I find amenities like cafes or accommodation near the hiking trails?
A: Bilhères is situated within the Ossau Valley, which offers various amenities. While specific cafes directly on every trail might be limited, the village of Bilhères itself and nearby towns in the valley provide options for accommodation, restaurants, and cafes. It's advisable to check local listings for current availability.

Q: Are there any nearby attractions to visit after a hike?
A: Absolutely! After your hike, you can explore several nearby attractions. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marie-Blanque Pass&lt;/a&gt; for more stunning mountain scenery, or the charming town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5588640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laruns&lt;/a&gt;. For a relaxing experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3453391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eaux-Bonnes Thermal Baths&lt;/a&gt; are also within reach.
tips: The Col de Marie-Blanque is an emblematic passage of the Tour de France which peaks at 1,035 meters above sea level. Its ascent from Louvie-Juzon is 15 kilometers long for an average of 4.1% with passages at 8.5%. Marie-Blanque marks the border between the beautiful valleys of Ossau and Aspe. In the middle of the climb, the pretty Bénou plateau offers a portion of respite in a remarkable setting.
The peak of Escurets is located at 1440 meters above sea level. It offers a splendid panorama over the valleys of Ossau and Aspe, but also over the Hautes-Pyrénées.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Pic d'Escurets Loop?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a varied landscape. You'll start on paths through pleasant undergrowth, then open up to pastures and ridges. Expect tracks, grassy paths, and a limestone plateau as you ascend. The Bénou plateau provides a beautiful, flatter section during the climb. Some parts, especially in foggy conditions, might involve less obvious paths.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and views along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the magnificent 360-degree panorama from the summit of Escurets Peak. On clear days, you can see towns like Pau and Tarbes, and prominent peaks such as Pic du Midi de Bigorre and Balaitous. The starting point, Marie-Blanque Pass, is also an iconic spot. The descent often provides beautiful views of Benou.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike the Pic d'Escurets Loop?
faqs.answer: While the trail can be enjoyed in various seasons, late spring to early autumn generally offers the most favorable conditions with clearer views and less chance of snow or ice on higher sections. Always check local weather forecasts before you go.

faqs.question: Is the Pic d'Escurets Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The Pic d'Escurets Loop is rated as moderate in difficulty. While it involves a significant elevation gain, it's considered accessible even if you're not an expert athlete, provided you take your time. It's not ideal for absolute beginners due to the elevation and varied terrain, but hikers with some experience should find it manageable.

faqs.question: Is the Pic d'Escurets Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally dog-friendly, but it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through pastures where livestock may be present, and to protect local wildlife. Always carry water for your dog and clean up after them.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Many variations of the hike, including this loop, typically start from the Marie-Blanque Pass (Col de Marie Blanque). There is usually parking available at this location, which serves as a common starting point for hikers in the area.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in the Vallée d'Ossau?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Pic d'Escurets Loop or generally within the Vallée d'Ossau. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning nature conservation and livestock.

faqs.question: Does the Pic d'Escurets Loop connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other established paths. You'll find it intersects with parts of "Le Pic d'Escurets", "La route des cols des Pyrénées de Larrau à Col d'Aubisque", and "Le Turon de Técouère", among others. This offers opportunities to explore further if you wish.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The area is known for its diverse wildlife. Keep an eye out for various bird species, including vultures, which are often spotted soaring above the peaks. You might also see local livestock in the pastures.

faqs.question: What is the historical significance of Pic d'Escurets?
faqs.answer: Beyond its natural beauty, Pic d'Escurets holds historical importance. In the 19th century, it was a crucial geodetic point used by military officers like Peytier and Hossard for the cartographic mapping of the Pyrenees, with a geodetic signal installed at its summit.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Direct public transport options to the Marie-Blanque Pass (Col de Marie Blanque) are limited. It is generally recommended to access the trailhead by car. Check local bus schedules for the Vallée d'Ossau region if you plan to rely on public transport, but expect to arrange for a taxi or a long walk for the final leg.
